titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5958

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=780 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.738932+00 | translation moa=781 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.748234+00

p.cl: Omnibus fidelibus has literas uisuris uel audituris  Ada filius Cospatricii de parua Ristona Salutem .

me intuitu karitatis et pro salute anime mee et predecessorum et successorum meorum dedis
se . concessisse . et presenti carta confirmasse in perpetuum in puram et perpetuam elemosinam deo et sancte Marie ad sustentationem
luminaris ubi celebratur missa de sancta Maria in ecclesia de Coldingham  toftum et croftum quod Huckard tenuit in
parua Ristona . Scilicet quod iacet inter toftum dominicum et toftum Willelmi filii Reginaldi . Libere . quiete . absolute ab omni
opere et seruicio seculari . et exactione et demanda . cum communi pastura . et cum omnibus libertatibus . et aysiamentis ad eandem
uillam pertinentibus . Infra uillam et extra . Ita libere et quiete sicut aliqua elemosinam in regno Scotie liberius . quiescius
et honorificentius tenetur uel possidetur . Ego uero et heredes mei predictum toftum et croftum contra omnes homines
et feminas warantizabimus .

Et ut hec mea donatio rata et inconcussa permaneat in perpetuum  presens scriptum
sigilli mei appositione roboraui .

Hiis Testibus . Domino Anketino priore de Coldingham . Iohanne . et Stephano
tunc Capellanis de Coldingham . Iohanne . filio Elye de Aytona . Roberto filio Gregorii . Willelmo Clerico . Ada tunc preposito .
Roberto filio Reginaldi . Rogero filio Radulfi . et multis aliis .
To all the faithful who shall view or hear these letters, Adam son of Cospatric of Little Reston sends greeting.

moved by charity and for the salvation of my own soul and of my predecessors' and successors', to have given, granted, and made firm by the present charter for ever, in pure and everlasting alms, to God and Saint Mary, for the maintenance of the light where the mass of Saint Mary is celebrated in the church of Coldingham, the toft and croft which Huckard held in in Little Reston; that is, which lies between the demesne toft and toft of William son of Reginald; freely, quitely, released from every work, secular service, exaction and demand, with common pasture, and with all liberties and easements pertaining to the same toun, within the toun and outside; so freely and quitely as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland is freely, quitely, and honourably held and possessed. I and my heirs shall warrant the aforesaid toft and croft against all men and women. So that this my donation should remain valid and unbroken for ever.
I have authenticated the present writing by the application of my seal.
